content:
# OOM Fix Series — Streaming Calibration + VRAM Management (2026-04-03)

PRs #36, #37, #38, #39 on chunzhe10/dorea

## Root causes and fixes

### PR #36 — Streaming paged calibration (RAM: ~27 GB → ~50 MB)
`auto_calibrate` accumulated all `CalibrationInput` frames in RAM simultaneously.
For 988 keyframes at 1024px proxy: `Vec<CalibrationInput>` + `keyframe_data_vec` (f32) + `all_depths` = ~27 GB peak.

Fix: `PagedCalibrationStore` writes each frame to disk as it arrives from inference.
Three streaming passes over mmapped files:
1. Depth-only reservoir sample (1M values, 4 MB) → adaptive zone boundaries
2. `StreamingLutBuilder::add_frame` one frame at a time → DepthLuts (~10 MB accumulators)
3. Apply LUT + derive HSL corrections per frame

Also added `StreamingLutBuilder` to `dorea-lut`; `build_depth_luts` now delegates to it.

### PR #37 — mmap packed files (I/O: 2964 syscalls → 2 mmap calls)
Per-frame individual file reads (988 × 3 = 2964 open/read/close) replaced with two packed binary files:
- `depths.bin` — all depth maps concatenated
- `pixtar.bin` — all [pixels | target] pairs concatenated

Both mmapped after inference phase. Pass 3 (HSL) hits pixtar.bin from OS page cache — ~0 disk I/O.

### PR #38 — Depth cache at proxy resolution (RAM: 29.7 GB → ~565 MB)
`keyframe_depths` HashMap stored depth maps upscaled to full video resolution (3840×2160).
With `--depth-max-interval 5` on 41s 4K clip: 941 keyframes × 31 MB = 29.7 GB → OOM.

Fix: store raw inference output at proxy resolution (~518×291, ~0.6 MB each).
Lerp at proxy resolution in pass 2, upscale result once per frame before GPU grader.
Quality identical — bilinear upscale commutes with linear interpolation.

### PR #39 — Shut down inference before NVENC init (VRAM: freed 1.5 GB)
`inf_server.shutdown()` was after pass 2, leaving Depth Anything V2 (~1.5 GB VRAM)
loaded throughout the encoding loop. NVENC 4K input buffer allocation failed with OOM.

Fix: shut down inference server immediately after batch depth inference completes.
All depths are in `keyframe_depths` at that point; subprocess no longer needed.

`shutdown()` is synchronous (blocks on `child.wait()`), so VRAM is guaranteed freed before returning.

## VRAM budget in pass 2 (grading + encoding)
- CUDA grader (PTX + LUT texture, pre-allocated buffers): ~300 MB
- NVENC 4K encoder input buffers: ~500 MB
- Total: ~800 MB — constant regardless of video length

## Depth cache scaling concern
At `--depth-max-interval 5`, depth HashMap scales linearly with clip length:
- 41s clip: 941 kf × 0.6 MB = ~565 MB ✓
- 2-hour dive: ~26k kf × 0.6 MB = ~15 GB — would be the next RAM ceiling
- Fix if needed: stream depth lookup in pass 2 (read from PagedCalibrationStore-style store)

## Keyframe extraction boundary fix (also in PR #36)
`ffprobe duration_secs` overshoots the last decodable frame. Fixed by computing
`safe_duration = (frame_count - 1) / fps` instead of using `duration_secs`.
